profile.personality: enthusiastic, makes more grammar slips, talks about football a lot

transcript.content: Hi Rohan, I hope your exam prep is going well. I’ve been a tea lover since monsoon season started. Did your practice of past tense feel easier this week?

Yeah, it's going well, thanks! The past tense is better now. I think I still mix a few words sometimes. Oh, I also watch some Bengali movie last night. It was so choti choti emotions type. Do you like movies?
You said "watch some Bengali movie last night", small fix: we say "watched a Bengali movie last night". What was the movie called?

Oh right, I forgot the "a" before movie. Thanks! The movie was called *Neel Aakrash*. It's a love story with lots of drama. You should watch it sometime! By the way, do you like football? I follow it a lot.
You said "I follow it a lot", and that's clear. *Neel Aakrash* sounds emotional, love stories with drama are my weakness. Yes, I like football! Which team do you support?

I support Manchester United! But I also like watching Real Madrid matches. Oh, and I take some photos during the games too. Do you watch football? My favorite player is Bruno Fernandes. Is there any team you like?

You said "take some photos", and that's clear. Bruno Fernandes is amazing, I love how he scores! I support Bengaluru FC, but I enjoy watching all Indian Super League matches. What kind of photos do you take?

I take photos of the players during the match, especially when they score a goal! And also some crowd shots, you know, the fans celebrating. I try to capture the emotions. Oh, and sometimes I take photos of the stadium lights during night matches. It looks so cool! I think I need to work on my camera settings more though. Do you take photos too?
That sounds fun. Capturing emotions and lights is great. I take photos too, but only of simple things like food or flowers. What kind of camera do you use?

I use a DSLR camera, but I still learn a lot. My friend teach me some basics. Oh, I also use my phone for quick shots. It's not as good, but it's easy to carry. Do you have any favorite photography moment? Maybe a photo you love the most?

You said "My friend teach me", small fix: we say "My friend teaches me". That’s a good setup, DSLR for details, phone for quick shots. I love photos of food and flowers, but I have no favorite, they all look the same to me! What’s your favorite photo you’ve taken?

Hmm, it’s hard to pick just one favorite photo. I love the one I took of a sunset during a local match—stadium lights and the golden sun together. It looked so magical! But I think my best photo is of a fan jumping with joy when his team scored. The expression was so real! Oh, maybe I should share it with you sometime. Do you take photos of people?

You said "looked so magical", and that's great. I take photos of people too, but only when they smile or laugh. The fan’s photo sounds powerful, emotions in a frame are the best. What made the fan jump like that?

The goal was the last one of the match, and the team was almost losing! The fan was wearing a scarf of the home team, so I think he was really emotional. It was like the whole stadium held its breath, and then he jumped—so much joy! I just snapped it quickly with my DSLR. Oh, and I try to use burst mode for such moments. But I still get blurry images sometimes. Anyway, it was a great shot! I should go now—got to read some more English grammar before sleep. Talk to you soon! 😊

You said "I try to use burst mode", and that’s clear. That sounds like a perfect moment to freeze, fans, goals, and emotions all in one frame! Talk to you soon, and good luck with your reading. Keep capturing those magic moments!

moments.text: When I speak English, my brain freezes and I mix up words. It makes me feel shy to speak.

They are best team in India
